delivery.raw: 主に対面（全開講回数の過半数）、一部オンライン

rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: Students will first explore the key theories of second language acquisition (SLA) before moving on to learn about factors that affect individual learners, and finally examine different teaching approaches in SLA.
aims: The aim of the course is to learn the fundamental theories and principles of second language acquisition. A further aim of the course is to help students gain a deeper understanding of the many factors at play during the process of acquiring a second language.
goals: Students will gain a deeper understanding of second language acquisition (SLA) theories and relevant terminology to discuss and write about SLA.
Students will learn and be able to make connections between theories and SLA learning factors with their own experiences.
Students will be able to make connections between theories and teaching approaches and their own language learning experience.

plan.text: Orientation and course outline. Introduction of some of the key concepts the course will cover. Main theme: What is second language acquisition (SLA)?
Main theme: First language acquisition versus second language acquisition
Main theme: The major theoretical approaches to SLA
Main theme: A continuation of the major theoretical approaches to SLA.
Main theme: An overview of some of the factors that affect second language learning
Main theme: A continuation of factors that affect second language learning
Main theme: Individual learner differences in second language acquisition
Main theme: Cognitive processes in SLA
Main theme: Cognitive processes in SLA continued
Main theme: The role of input and interaction in SLA
Main theme: The role of input and interaction in SLA continued
Main theme: Teaching approaches in SLA
Main theme: Teaching approaches in SLA continued
Main theme: A continuation of teaching approaches and final examination review

textbooks: There is no textbook for this class. All materials (readings, handouts) will be made available to the class.
prereq: There are no prerequisites for this course, but students should have a keen interest in the field of second language acquisition.
prep: Please make sure to review the content of each class and complete all assigned readings.

extra.text: 本授業の内容は、文化審議会国語分科会『日本語教育人材の養成・研修の在り方について（報告）改訂版』に示された「必須の教育内容」50項目のうち、(15)言語学習、(16)習得過程、(17)学習ストラテジー、(19)日本語の学習・教育の情意的側面、(29)中間言語分析、(34)コミュニケーション教育に対応するものです。
